articles.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
articles.summary: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.

articles.title: Eric Swalwell Faces Sex Assault Accusation as He Runs for California Governor
articles.summary: Eric Swalwell, a Democratic congressman, denied the accounts published by CNN and The San Francisco Chronicle. Nancy Pelosi was among those who said he should withdraw from the California governor’s r

articles.title: Months after the ICE shootings in Minnesota, a federal probe remains elusive
articles.summary: Officials in Minnesota have sued the Trump administration, saying federal officials are withholding evidence in the killings of U.S. citizens Alex Pretti and Renee Macklin Good by immigration agents i

articles.title: Iran Unable to Find Mines It Planted in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Says
articles.summary: The lost mines have prevented Iran from quickly complying with President Trump’s demand to allow more ships to pass through the waterway.

articles.title: How Recovery Personnel Will Secure Artemis II Capsule at Sea After Splashdown
articles.summary: As the spacecraft bobs in the Pacific Ocean, recovery personnel from NASA and the U.S. Navy are out at sea, tracking the capsule and other pieces of jettisoned hardware.

articles.title: See Photos From All 10 Days of NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission
articles.summary: Over 10 days, the astronauts of NASA’s lunar flyby mission have achieved the near impossible.

articles.title: World Leaders Push to Save Iran Talks Amid Israel’s Attacks in Lebanon
articles.summary: President Trump’s cease-fire with Iran appears at risk as Vice President JD Vance heads to Pakistan for discussions with Iranian officials.

articles.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
articles.summary: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Ira

articles.title: WATCH LIVE: Artemis II astronauts have splashed down on Earth
articles.summary: The four astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II mission are about to plunge through the atmosphere toward Earth, after a successful visit to the moon.

articles.title: Trump Is Racing to Redefine ‘America First’ in a Time of War
articles.summary: Washington has been debating the thrust of Mr. Trump’s foreign policy for more than a decade, with the only consensus being that “America First” means whatever the president says it does.

articles.title: After more than 9 days in flight, NASA's Artemis II is set to return to Earth
articles.summary: The four astronauts on NASA's Artemis II lunar mission are set to return to Earth Friday evening. The crew is wrapping up a journey around the moon with a planned splashdown off San Diego, Calif.

articles.title: Facing Many Crises, Pakistan Tries to End a Big One — in Iran
articles.summary: A nation with a stalled economy, a terrorism problem and two hostile neighbors is set to host the first formal U.S.-Iranian talks since the war began.

articles.title: Epstein survivors have mixed feelings on Melania Trump's call for hearing in Congress
articles.summary: The first lady made a public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with Epstein, and calling for further action in Congress. Survivors of the late sex offender's abuse differ on her proposa

articles.title: Shortlisted for an Oscar, 'Homebound' is a daring movie about two dear friends
articles.summary: The movie, now streaming on Netflix, defied current trends in Indian cinema to tell the true story of a friendship between a Muslim and a Hindu Dalit. Martin Scorsese was secretly involved.

articles.title: Five things to know about Hungary's election
articles.summary: Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.

articles.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue
articles.summary: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.

articles.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
articles.summary: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years.  Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

articles.title: VP Vance to lead U.S. team in Iran peace talks. And, Artemis II to return to Earth
articles.summary: Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.

articles.title: Iran delegates arrive in Islamabad for talks with U.S., as ceasefire terms remain in dispute
articles.summary: The fragile ceasefire agreement was tested again on Friday after Iran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Hezbollah traded strikes in Lebanon, and Kuwait was attacked with drones.

articles.title: Vance heads to Pakistan for talks to end the war between Iran, the US and Israel
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance enters his highest-profile moment Friday as lead U.S. negotiator in face-to-face talks with Iran.

articles.title: India cracks down on social media jokes criticizing prime minister
articles.summary: As criticism of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's response to the Iran war grows, government censors are scrambling to knock down memes that use the Indian leader as the punchline.

articles.title: How governments have tried to hide information about the Iran war online
articles.summary: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
